features.summary: Raytheon Technologies reported strong Q2 2021 results with sales of $15.9 billion, up 10% organically year-over-year, and adjusted EPS of $1.03 exceeding expectations. Free cash flow of $966 million also beat forecasts. The company raised full-year guidance, increasing the EPS range to $3.85-$4.00 and free cash flow to $4.5-$5.0 billion. Commercial aerospace recovery drove outperformance, particularly in aftermarket sales which grew 24% at Collins, while defense bookings remained strong with over $4 billion in awards including classified programs.
features.management_outlook: Management expects commercial air traffic to return to 2019 levels by end of 2023, with domestic and narrow-body recovery preceding international and wide-body segments. They anticipate low-to-mid single digit organic growth in defense businesses through 2025, supported by strong $66 billion backlog. The company increased merger synergy targets to $1.5 billion and remains confident in achieving 2025 goals of strong top-line growth, margin expansion, and at least $10 billion in free cash flow while returning $20 billion to shareholders in the first four years post-merger.
features.risks: Key risks include slower-than-expected international border reopenings and long-haul route recovery, which could impact wide-body aftermarket demand that represents 40-45% of Collins aftermarket business. The company faces potential R&D tax amortization headwinds starting in 2022 without Congressional relief. Production issues at Boeing's 787 program and slower ramp rates could affect OE revenues, while absorption headwinds from lower volumes continue to pressure Pratt & Whitney's GTF engine margins.
features.key_drivers: Commercial aftermarket recovery is the primary growth driver, with Collins aftermarket up 24% year-over-year and showing 15% sequential growth. Defense modernization funding remains at historic highs with favorable budget allocation for RTX programs including missile defense, space systems, and hypersonics. Operational excellence initiatives are delivering $50 million in productivity improvements across defense segments, while cost reduction actions and favorable contract settlements are expanding margins across the portfolio.
